reasoning: Saint Pierre and Miquelon is a highly peaceful French overseas territory with a population of approximately 6,000. It has no organized armed groups or active armed conflicts. The ACLED event category "Violence against civilians" specifically requires an organized armed group inflicting violence. Since the baseline (reference value) is 1.000, zero such events occurred in the past 360 days. To meet the resolution criteria locally, at least 11 organized violence events against civilians would need to happen within a 30-day window prior to each resolution date. The likelihood of an unprecedented insurgency breaking out and causing 11+ recorded events within any 30-day window in this tiny territory is astronomically small. The probabilities are kept at near-minimum bounds but increase incrementally for the later dates to correctly reflect increasing epistemic uncertainty over longer time horizons.
